page: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-300s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 16 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. The instance is powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This CPU configuration features 16 physical cores with a single thread per core, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. Local storage is provided via a bundled 300 GB NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a balanced performance profile. The AMD EPYC-Rome processor delivers strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and database operations fall within the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both read and write operations also register in the middle 50% range. Notably, the server demonstrates strong text generation speeds for medium (7B) LLM models and top-tier performance for small (135M) LLM text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio position this server as a resource-dense option for compute-focused workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The combination of 16 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and local NVMe storage makes this instance suitable for multi-threaded decompression tasks, web serving, database operations, and moderate machine learning inference workloads.
description: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-300s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core, and includes a bundled 300 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database performance remain in the average tier. Lacking a dedicated GPU, this x86_64 instance relies on its 16 physical cores to handle workloads. The shared CPU model offers a cost-effective resource profile for compute-focused applications. This server is well-suited for web serving, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and moderate CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-16c-32gb-300s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 300 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core and average multi-core performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized shared instance featuring sixteen AMD EPYC cores and local NVMe storage for balanced processing and database workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory and a 300 GB NVMe SSD
